\boxed{B}

The Hawthorn Studies are most closely associated with **Elton Mayo**, who led the research at the Western Electric Hawthorn Works plant in the 1920s–30s. These studies explored human behavior, worker motivation, and the "Hawthorne Effect," emphasizing social and psychological factors in productivity.
